mission.title: prometheus: Configurable limit for Prometheus's disk space usage
mission.description: Configurable limit for Prometheus's disk space usage. Requested by 16+ users.

steps.title: Check current prometheus deployment
steps.description:
Verify your prometheus version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
helm list -n prometheus 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working prometheus installation.

steps.title: Review prometheus configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant prometheus configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
kubectl get configmap -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=prometheus
```
Prometheus needs a startup flag or HTTP endpoint to trigger an immediate maintenance sweep to delete old files from local storage.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Configurable limit for Prometheus's disk space usage
steps.description:
Thinking about this, I'm not sure if catering for the "disk completely full" case makes a lot of sense. Experience tells us that the storage is often in very sorry state if Prometheus runs into a disk-full scenario. You probably cannot shut down cleanly in that state, and the crash recovery will need its own share of disk, so an immediate deletion of time series will only help in the cases where you have just enough disk space for crash recovery but not enough to wait for normal purging...

I

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
kubectl get events -n prometheus --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Configurable limit for Prometheus's disk space usage" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Thinking about this, I'm not sure if catering for the "disk completely full" case makes a lot of sense. Experience tells us that the storage is often in very sorry state if Prometheus runs into a disk-full scenario.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with prometheus installed or the issue environment reproducible.
